title: Configure Anthropic WIF for verification research
description: Replace the static verifier key fallback with short-lived Anthropic workload-identity federation. One-time external setup: create an Anthropic service account/federation rule scoped to repo:PaulDuvall/ai-development-patterns, set EVIDENCE_ANTHROPIC_FEDERATION_RULE_ID, EVIDENCE_ANTHROPIC_ORGANIZATION_ID, and optional EVIDENCE_ANTHROPIC_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_ID/EVIDENCE_ANTHROPIC_WORKSPACE_ID repository variables, validate only through a newly approved gated run, and keep the retired generic ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and unprefixed variables absent.
acceptance_criteria: A newly dispatched verify-patterns workflow attempt is explicitly approved and authenticates through EVIDENCE_ANTHROPIC_* workload identity; no static EVIDENCE_ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is needed; retired generic Anthropic credentials remain absent; candidate secret scans and read-only GitHub token isolation pass.
notes: Run 29104416684 stopped before candidate upload with authentication_failed. The legacy ANTHROPIC_API_KEY has now been deleted and no federation variables are configured. Do not re-run the historical attempt or set unprefixed ANTHROPIC_* variables; historical workflow definitions can read current repository credentials. Complete Anthropic Console WIF setup under the EVIDENCE_ANTHROPIC_* names, then use a fresh main-branch dispatch and approve its evidence-paid-research plan.
close_reason: Superseded by local-only Codex agent evaluation; GitHub-hosted model research and its Anthropic credentials are being removed.

title: Simplify verification docs: single source of truth + rename 'verified' field
description: Obtuseness fixes from 2026-07-04 review, all rigor-preserving: (1) Rename evidence field 'verified:' (a date) to 'last_checked:' — collides with 'verdict: verified'; a weak pattern would read 'verified: <date> / verdict: unverified'. Inventory calls the same value 'last_verified'. Cheap NOW while PRs #18-#28 are unmerged; schema migration later. (2) Rubric stated 3x (verification/README.md, verify-patterns.md, validator constants) — make README the human-readable source, command file references it, validator enforces; optionally a drift test. (3) Add 6-line 'How it works' flow at top of verification/README.md: patterns.yaml -> inventory -> 3-mode search -> independent grader re-fetches URLs -> script computes score/verdict -> PRs+issues, humans decide. (4) Root README + CLAUDE.md repo-structure tree never mention verification/ (checked all PR branches too) — add one-line pointers. (5) Replace '(undated page; date = retrieval date)' prose convention (22 occurrences, 9/10 files, inconsistently applied) with machine-checkable date_basis field or make date optional. (6) Pipeline writes 2-line header comment into generated evidence YAML explaining scale + do-not-hand-edit.

title: Replace OpenAI API key with GitHub OIDC federation
description: Migrate the OpenAI verification provider from the OPENAI_API_KEY secret to short-lived GitHub Actions workload identity federation when the Codex Action accepts an exchanged token, or through a reviewed SDK/adapter that preserves the isolated candidate boundary.
acceptance_criteria: The OpenAI research job authenticates from GitHub OIDC with repository/ref/workflow assertions and api.model.request scope; no static OPENAI_API_KEY remains; the on-demand OpenAI verification run passes all isolation and provenance gates.
close_reason: Superseded by local-only Codex agent evaluation; GitHub-hosted OpenAI authentication is no longer part of the design.

title: Audit PR1: delete .ai/ tree and leftover rename/merged-pattern example dirs (R3+R5)
description: From AUDIT-RECOMMENDATIONS.md 2026-07-11 audit. R3: git rm -r .ai/ (12 tracked files, zero consumers), remove stale .gitignore entries, rm -rf 5 untracked leftover rename dirs. R5: fold examples/automated-traceability real scripts into examples/spec-driven-development and delete dir; move examples/baseline-management/chaos to examples/guided-chaos, update README.md:2957 link, delete rest. Acceptance: guards pass (validate-pattern-names.py + pytest -m 'not slow'), PR merged.
close_reason: PR1 merged as #81 (commit d4d8b26): .ai/ tree deleted, leftover rename dirs removed, automated-traceability folded into spec-driven-development, guided-chaos example created

title: Fix verify-patterns correctness bugs (pre-merge, on the open PR branches)
description: From 2026-07-04 tooling review (42 confirmed findings). Fix before/while merging PRs #18-#30: (1) verification/README.md:18 states the INVERSE of the central rule — 'verified with naming_alignment: none means real practice, our name for it' should read 'NOT our name for it'. (2) Canonical YAML example in .claude/commands/verify-patterns.md:113-140 fails its own validator: 2 entries sum to 7 not 14, alignment recomputes weak not strong, verdict recomputes weak not verified. (3) Tier-cap semantics: README:24 'at most 3 entries count per tier' implies score-cap; validate-evidence.py rejects >3 as error and sums uncapped — align wording to 'max 3 admitted; keep the 3 strongest'. (4) validate-evidence.py fails open: slug:null/verified:null bypass filename+date checks (line 110/112 truthiness guards); regex-only ISO dates accept 2026-13-45 when quoted; evidence:[] is an undocumented second empty encoding; no URL-shape check ('https://...' placeholder passes). (5) test_evidence_files.py: add score-mismatch, naming-mismatch, slug/filename-mismatch tests; replace directory pytest.skip with hard assert after #18+#19 merge.

title: Harden verify-patterns workflow ops (scheduling, dedup, bounds)
description: From loop-seams review: (1) Cross-run dedup: prioritization reads main, PR-dedup covers same slug+date only — next Monday cron re-selects the same 10 patterns and opens duplicate verify/<slug>-<newdate> PRs alongside #19-#28. Phase 1 should treat slugs with an open verify/* PR as in-flight and skip. (2) Phase 4 discovery NEVER runs on schedule (cron is stale-default only) — add monthly discover-only cron. (3) Scheduled runs hard-fail while VERIFY_PATTERNS_PAT is unset with no tracked signal — add notify-on-failure issue step (note: pattern-validation's notify-failure is push-scoped, doesn't cover schedule either). (4) Scale max-turns by mode; agent should write selected work-list to a file at Phase 1 so the always() summary step can diff selected-vs-opened PRs deterministically (truncation report currently written by the agent that gets cut off). (5) Validate workflow_dispatch 'pattern' input against patterns.yaml in a shell step before it reaches the write-capable agent's prompt (injection surface). (6) always() step listing verify/*,discover/* branches with no open PR (mid-run cutoff strands them). (7) Change Phase 0 'create the guard if missing' to 'abort and tell human to merge guard PR' once #18 merges. (8) Evidence URLs never re-checked deterministically — extend the slow link-check pattern to verification/evidence/*.yaml. (9) Clarify default-mode selection at steady state: 'up to 10 patterns missing-or-older-than stale-days; fewer qualify -> verify only those'.

title: Make evidence validator catalog-aware + settle pattern-inventory.yaml
description: The single change that delivers 'updates with every catalog change': validate-evidence.py (or test_evidence_files.py) cross-checks slugs BOTH directions against patterns.yaml + experiments inventory — (a) every evidence slug must exist in the registry (orphan detection on rename/delete), (b) every registry slug must have an evidence file or be on an explicit not-yet-verified allowlist (so adding a pattern forces a visible diff). Also settle pattern-inventory.yaml: it is regenerated in Phase 1 BEFORE evidence is written and no Phase-3 PR lane refreshes it, so the committed copy (riding in PR #18) is permanently stale (all 49 show evidence_count:0 while sibling PRs carry verified evidence). Recommend: stop committing it (gitignore, document as ephemeral per-run working file); alternative: regenerate at end of run inside the batched sources PR.

title: Apply evidence-led two-word pattern taxonomy migration
description: Use pattern-spec.md as the naming/structure authority. Apply the approved stable and experimental renames, demotions, promotions, consolidations, retirements, definition corrections, aliases, and upstream/downstream migrations across registry, docs, examples, evidence, decisions, generated status/site data, tests, and tracking. Validate and land on main.
acceptance_criteria: Every canonical pattern name is exactly two words; all catalog boundaries and maturity changes are applied; retired/merged patterns have migration paths; no stale names, anchors, paths, dependencies, evidence mappings, or generated data remain; full local and GitHub checks pass; changes are merged to main.
close_reason: Applied the evidence-led exact two-word taxonomy across all 47 active patterns, examples, evidence, generated data, validators, workflows, tests, and documentation; all local gates pass and Dependency Migration evidence is tracked separately in ai-development-patterns-4l3.

title: Move pattern evaluation to local Codex agents
description: Remove model-backed pattern evaluation from GitHub Actions and replace it with a repository-local Codex skill that uses signed-in local agent/subagent sessions. Keep deterministic evidence validation and weekly link/freshness checks in Actions, support explicit stable (24), exploratory (25), all (49), stale, single-pattern, and discovery scopes, and add local provenance accepted by the deterministic validator.
acceptance_criteria: No GitHub workflow can invoke a model-backed pattern evaluator or reference evaluator API credentials; verify-patterns and the general hosted assistant are deleted and disabled; local $evaluate-pattern-adoption planning uses a current clean origin/main base, checks open evidence PRs, shows exact scope/cost bounds, requires exact human authorization before fan-out, runs through at most three read-only local subagents without API keys, supports content-bound codex-local provenance, validates every output, and requires separate draft-PR approval.
notes: Implemented on codex/local-agent-pattern-evaluation. Supported non-slow repository suite: 318 passed. Both independent security and correctness re-audits report no remaining P0/P1 findings. Live verify-patterns and general Claude assistant workflows are disabled; evaluator secrets/variables are absent.
close_reason: Implemented and fully validated local-only Codex agent evaluation, deterministic-only Actions, live workflow containment, exact human gates, local provenance, documentation, and cost/scope guardrails.

title: Resolve open dependency PRs and Dependabot alerts
description: Consolidate every distinct open Dependabot dependency and GitHub Actions update, remediate all open security alerts without unsupported dismissals, validate the combined change, merge it to main, close superseded duplicate PRs, and verify both queues are empty.
close_reason: Merged rollup PR #78 and compatibility PR #79; closed all 13 superseded Dependabot PRs; GitHub reports zero open PRs and zero open Dependabot alerts; alerts #13-#17 are fixed without dismissal; local and post-merge validation passed.

title: Require human approval before paid evidence research
description: Gate every scheduled and manual paid evidence run behind a GitHub Actions environment approval after the free deterministic planning and cost-summary step. No OpenAI or Anthropic preflight or research call may execute before approval. Configure the environment to require the repository owner as reviewer and document the approval/rejection flow.
acceptance_criteria: All provider preflight and research jobs transitively depend on one environment-gated authorization job; scheduled and manual runs display the selected units and cost warning before approval; CI tests enforce the dependency; the GitHub environment requires a human reviewer; and a rejected/unapproved run makes zero provider calls.
notes: Immediate containment applied: verify-patterns workflow disabled_manually; evidence-paid-research environment requires PaulDuvall, protected branches only, self-review allowed for sole owner, and administrator bypass disabled. Legacy OPENAI_API_KEY repository secret deleted so historical workflow attempts cannot spend. Guard code binds approval to exact plan/run attempt, verifies live environment settings fail-closed, rotates future OpenAI use to EVIDENCE_OPENAI_API_KEY, and documents operator review. Local suite: 321 passed, 3 deselected.
close_reason: Merged PR #71; live workflow requires owner approval for each paid plan/run attempt, admin bypass is disabled, and the legacy OpenAI secret is deleted.

title: Make validation governance fail closed and efficient
description: Require the general deterministic validation gate alongside Trusted evidence checks; replace redundant test reruns with a result-only gate; eliminate empty artifact uploads, move slow links to scheduled/manual runs, add caching/concurrency/path scoping, and make closed-PR trusted validation a clean no-op.
close_reason: Merged PR #83: consolidated deterministic Validation gate, trusted validation, caching, scoped triggers, and scheduled network checks

title: Evaluate Dependency Migration adoption evidence locally
description: Use the evaluate-pattern-adoption skill in a signed-in local Codex session, select the single Dependency Migration pattern, obtain explicit plan and final-diff human approvals, and create a reviewable evidence PR. Do not run model-backed research in GitHub Actions or with a provider API key.
acceptance_criteria: verification/evidence/dependency-migration.yaml is independently researched and deterministically valid; dependency-migration is removed atomically from verification/pending-evidence.yaml; STATUS.md is regenerated; local approval/provenance records and all checks pass; a draft evidence PR is submitted for human review without auto-merge.

title: Promote Event Automation from experiments to main patterns
description:
## Goal
Promote the **Event Automation** experimental pattern to a stable, top-level pattern.

## References
- `pattern-spec.md` (required template + naming/anchor rules)
- `PATTERN_MIGRATION_GUIDE.md` (canonical naming + anchor conventions)

> Note: `PATTERN_RENAME_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` was not found in the repo root; follow the naming rules above.

## Pattern Content Promotion
- Move example implementation: `experiments/examples/event-automation/` → `examples/event-automation/`.
- Review and update internal links inside the moved example docs so they resolve to the new canonical section in root `README.md`.
- Promote the pattern narrative currently in `experiments/README.md` (`### Event Automation`) into main `README.md` under `# Development Patterns`.
- Ensure the promoted pattern section conforms to the `pattern-spec.md` structure:
  - **Maturity**, **Description**, **Related Patterns** (hyperlinked)
  - Implementation content + at least one **Anti-pattern**
  - **Complete Implementation** link to `examples/event-automation/`
- Assign maturity/type (expected: **Intermediate**, **Development**).

## Root README.md Updates (`README.md`)
- Add a new row to **Complete Pattern Reference** table:
  - Pattern: `Event Automation`
  - Maturity/Type/Description/Dependencies
  - Ensure dependencies are stable patterns only (likely `Codified Rules`, `Security Sandbox`).
- Add the full **Event Automation** section under `# Development Patterns`.
- Update **Pattern Dependencies & Implementation Order** (`graph TD` block) to include Event Automation where appropriate.
- Update contextual guidance sections if applicable:
  - `### Context-Based Pattern Selection` (Development Teams / Enterprise)
  - `### Project Type Recommendations`
  - `### Team Size Considerations`
- Update pattern count badges after updating tests via `python3 scripts/update-pattern-count.py` (updates `README.md` and `index.html`).

## Cross-Reference Updates
- Remove Event Automation from:
  - `experiments/README.md` reference table and its full pattern section.
  - `experiments/examples/README.md` example catalog table.
- Update references in docs:
  - `docs/ai-development-best-practices.md`: update the Event Automation link from `../experiments/README.md#event-automation` → `../README.md#event-automation` (or the appropriate relative path).
- Update **Related Patterns** in other main patterns that should reference Event Automation (edit relevant sections in `README.md`):
  - [Codified Rules](README.md#codified-rules)
  - [Security Sandbox](README.md#security-sandbox)
  - [Custom Commands](README.md#custom-commands) once promoted
  - [Progressive Disclosure](README.md#progressive-disclosure) once promoted
- Repo-wide search to update any remaining links:
  - Search for `Event Automation`, `#event-automation`, and `experiments/README.md#event-automation`.

## Supporting File Updates
- Tests / validation lists:
  - `tests/conftest.py`: add **Event Automation** to `EXPECTED_PATTERNS` and `PATTERN_CATEGORIES` (Development).
- Ensure CI stays green:
  - `.github/workflows/pattern-validation.yml` (no change expected, but confirm)
  - Run local suite (at minimum): `pytest -q`
- Check for references in config:
  - `CLAUDE.md`
  - `.ai/` rules/prompts

## Acceptance Criteria
- Event Automation is documented in `README.md` (table + full pattern section) and removed from `experiments/README.md`.
- Example implementation is moved to `examples/event-automation/` and removed from `experiments/examples/`.
- `docs/ai-development-best-practices.md` links to `README.md#event-automation` (not experimental anchors).
- No remaining links reference `experiments/README.md#event-automation`; full `pattern-validation.yml` test suite passes.

title: Repair embedded example quality gates
description: Repair the embedded Spec-Driven Development quality gates and shell examples: enforce real coverage, bind requirement references to collected pytest nodes, reject invalid resources without mutation, generate valid S3 action/resource pairs, and make setup/pre-commit instructions reproducible.
acceptance_criteria: All tracked shell scripts pass bash -n and ShellCheck at error severity; the embedded suite enforces its documented coverage and traceability thresholds; production/non-collected/mismatched test references fail closed; invalid resources are rejected; S3 policies pair bucket and object actions with valid ARNs; setup and hooks work from the repository root; repository gates pass.
close_reason: Repaired and enforced embedded spec coverage, collected-node traceability, IAM resource semantics, maintenance hooks, and shell gates; clean pinned suites pass.

title: Add reproducible npm dependency maintenance for examples
description: Decide and implement lockfiles plus Dependabot npm coverage for examples/centralized-rules/gateway-strategy/{ai-dev-cli,ai-gateway,org-ai-client}. The three package.json files currently have no lockfiles; GitHub reports no open alerts, but transitive resolution is not reproducible or continuously updated.
close_reason: Merged PR #83: all three npm examples have lockfiles, builds, audits, and Dependabot coverage

title: Audit PR2: fix catalog contradictions + purge retired pattern names (R6+R7)
description: From AUDIT-RECOMMENDATIONS.md. R6: Issue Generation sizing canonicalized to <1h at 5 locations + patterns.yaml; reconcile Dependencies column vs mermaid diagrams (Planned Implementation, Code Research, Debt Forecasting); Related Patterns omissions; evergreen violations. R7: rename retired pattern names in 6 example files; fix 5 PATTERN_MIGRATION_GUIDE anchor rows; record historical-document link exemption. Acceptance: guards pass, PR merged.
close_reason: PR2 merged as #82: sizing canonicalized to <1h, diagrams reconciled with table, retired names purged, migration-guide anchors flattened, evergreen fixes

title: Isolate provider credentials from historical workflow reruns
description: Replace shared legacy provider secret references with capability-specific names so historical verify-patterns definitions cannot regain credentials. Keep current paid validator runs behind the evidence-paid-research approval gate and make optional Claude workflows skip cleanly when their dedicated secrets are absent.
acceptance_criteria: No workflow references secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY or secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY; validator uses EVIDENCE_* secrets; optional Claude assistant/review use separate secrets and skip without them; legacy repository secrets remain deleted; tests and docs enforce the boundary.
notes: Live Actions secrets are empty and legacy Anthropic federation variables are absent. Parallel audit confirmed historical pre-gate runs could read current generic credentials; capability-specific EVIDENCE_ANTHROPIC_*, CLAUDE_ASSISTANT_API_KEY, and CLAUDE_REVIEW_API_KEY names close that path.
close_reason: Merged PR #73; legacy provider credentials are deleted, capability-specific names are enforced, optional Claude spend is opt-in, and live repository controls were reapplied successfully.

title: Capture a trusted verification search ledger
description: Record local agent search/tool events outside model-authored evidence and reconcile each complete evidence file query/candidate record against a sanitized ledger bound to the approved codex-local run manifest and research-contract digest. This closes the remaining gap where schema-valid query strings and candidate counts prove a recorded search plan but not that every claimed search executed.
acceptance_criteria: Each local evaluation run emits a trusted, sanitized search ledger bound to its codex-local run ID, approved manifest digest, and research-contract digest; validation rejects missing/extra/mismatched search modes, queries, or candidate counts; terminal evidence: none found cannot become complete without a reconciled three-mode ledger; raw reasoning and credentials are never retained.
close_reason: Merged PR #83: approved local runs now emit and reconcile a sanitized hash-chained search ledger

title: Review naming recommendations for 9 assessed patterns
description: Review the evidence-derived rename/alias recommendations retained in verification/DECISIONS.md. Current assessments are schema-v2 legacy imports and compute to weak until refreshed, so accept naming changes only after complete evidence refresh. PATTERN_MIGRATION_GUIDE.md governs accepted renames.
close_reason: Maintainer accepted the three remaining recommendations; canonical names, evidence mappings, docs, examples, tests, decisions, status, and generated site artifacts migrated.

title: Correct Docker dependency upgrades and add build coverage
description: Supersede Dependabot PR #84 with supported Python and Node base images, a valid parallel-agent build context, checked-in provider-free simulator entrypoints, synchronized documentation, and explicit Docker compatibility coverage that remains outside the deterministic required gate.
acceptance_criteria: Both image tags and Node are supported; every COPY/entrypoint exists; docs and Compose match the runtime; deterministic repository tests cover structure and behavior; a non-required network-backed workflow builds and smoke-tests both images on relevant changes, weekly, and on demand; no provider credentials or model calls are possible; repository gates pass; replacement PR merges and #84 closes.
close_reason: Implemented supported Docker runtimes, provider-free runnable fixtures, hardened sandbox lifecycles, and optional weekly/manual compatibility coverage; local and live gates pass.

title: Remove unavailable GitHub Models attestation and document enforcement boundary
description: Remove the nonexistent repository settings/models prerequisite, retain workflow-level Models/provider rejection, document the real reviewed-workflow versus account-wide spend boundary, update tests, and verify/apply the live three-check ruleset.
close_reason: Removed unavailable Models attestation, documented exact enforcement and billing boundaries, fixed CodeQL retained-state verification, passed 499 tests, and applied/verified live controls including all three required checks.

title: Promote Custom Commands from experiments to main patterns
description:

- No remaining links reference `experiments/README.md#custom-commands`; full `pattern-validation.yml` test suite passes.

title: Promote Image Spec from experiments to main patterns
description:
## Goal
Promote the **Image Spec** experimental pattern to a stable, top-level pattern.

## References
- `pattern-spec.md` (required template + naming/anchor rules)
- `PATTERN_MIGRATION_GUIDE.md` (canonical naming + anchor conventions)

> Note: `PATTERN_RENAME_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` was not found in the repo root; follow the naming rules above.

## Pattern Content Promotion
- Promote the pattern narrative currently in `experiments/README.md` (`### Image Spec`) into main `README.md` under `# Development Patterns`.
- Create a new complete implementation directory (since no experimental example dir exists today):
  - Add `examples/image-spec/README.md` with concrete, repeatable workflows (attach diagram → prompt template → iteration loop).
  - Include lightweight sample assets (e.g., simple SVG/PNG diagrams) if appropriate, or document how to generate them.
- Ensure the promoted pattern section conforms to the `pattern-spec.md` structure:
  - **Maturity**, **Description**, **Related Patterns** (hyperlinked)
  - Implementation content + at least one **Anti-pattern**
  - **Complete Implementation** link to `examples/image-spec/`
- Assign maturity/type (expected: **Intermediate**, **Development**).
- Reconcile dependencies so the **main** reference table does not depend on experimental-only patterns (e.g., `Context Optimization`). If needed, keep experimental patterns as non-dependency “Related Patterns” links instead.

## Root README.md Updates (`README.md`)
- Add a new row to **Complete Pattern Reference** table:
  - Pattern: `Image Spec`
  - Maturity/Type/Description/Dependencies (dependencies must be patterns in main `README.md`)
- Add the full **Image Spec** section under `# Development Patterns`.
- Update **Pattern Dependencies & Implementation Order** (`graph TD` block) if Image Spec changes recommended sequencing.
- Update contextual guidance sections if applicable:

  - `### Team Size Considerations`
- Update pattern count badges after updating tests via `python3 scripts/update-pattern-count.py` (updates `README.md` and `index.html`).

## Cross-Reference Updates

- Update references in docs:

## Supporting File Updates

- Ensure CI stays green:
  - `.github/workflows/pattern-validation.yml` (no change expected, but confirm)

- Check for references in config:
  - `CLAUDE.md`
  - `.ai/` rules/prompts

## Acceptance Criteria

- `examples/image-spec/` exists with a usable, repo-appropriate “complete implementation” (docs + sample assets) and is linked from the pattern section.
- `docs/ai-development-best-practices.md` links to `README.md#image-spec` (not experimental anchors).
- Main reference-table dependencies do not require experimental-only patterns; full `pattern-validation.yml` test suite passes.

title: Verify migration of 5 experimental patterns to main
description:
## Goal
Run end-to-end verification after promoting these 5 patterns from `experiments/` into the main catalog:
- Centralized Rules
- Progressive Disclosure
- Event Automation
- Custom Commands
- Image Spec

## Validation Checklist
### Links & Anchors
- Run internal link validation:
  - `pytest tests/test_links.py -q`
  - `pytest tests/test_readme_accuracy.py -q`
- Repo-wide grep for stale experimental links:
  - `rg "experiments/README.md#(centralized-rules|progressive-disclosure|event-automation|custom-commands|image-spec)" -S .`

### Mermaid Diagrams
- Confirm Mermaid syntax tests pass:
  - `pytest tests/test_diagram.py -q`
- Manually spot-check rendering on GitHub for:
  - `README.md` top overview graph
  - `README.md` “Pattern Dependencies & Implementation Order” diagram
  - Any new diagrams added during promotion

### Pattern Count + Reference Table
- Verify pattern count badge is correct and in sync:
  - `python3 scripts/update-pattern-count.py`
  - Ensure `git diff --exit-code README.md index.html`
- Verify `README.md` Complete Pattern Reference table matches implemented sections:
  - `pytest tests/test_pattern_compliance.py -q`
  - `pytest tests/test_readme_accuracy.py -q`

### Example Directories
- Validate examples are healthy after moving/adding directories:
  - `pytest tests/test_examples.py -q`
- Confirm example directories moved from `experiments/examples/` to `examples/` and all links still resolve.

### Dependencies
- Validate no missing or circular dependencies:
  - `pytest tests/test_dependencies.py -q`
- Confirm dependencies for promoted patterns reference only main patterns (no experimental-only dependencies in the main reference table).

### Experiments Catalog Hygiene
- Confirm `experiments/README.md`:
  - No longer lists the 5 promoted patterns in the experimental reference table.
  - No longer contains the full pattern sections for the 5 patterns.
- Confirm `experiments/examples/README.md`:
  - No longer lists example directories for Centralized Rules / Event Automation / Custom Commands.

### CI Workflow Parity
- Ensure local results match what `.github/workflows/pattern-validation.yml` runs (pattern compliance, readme accuracy, link validation, example validation, dependencies, diagram validation).

## Acceptance Criteria
- All internal links and anchors resolve (no broken links/anchors in `README.md`, `docs/`, `examples/`).
- Mermaid diagrams render without syntax errors; `pytest tests/test_diagram.py` passes.
- Pattern count badge matches the updated `tests/conftest.py` count; `python3 scripts/update-pattern-count.py` produces no diff.
- Full local test suite passes (equivalent to `.github/workflows/pattern-validation.yml`).
- `experiments/README.md` and `experiments/examples/README.md` no longer list: Centralized Rules, Progressive Disclosure, Event Automation, Custom Commands, Image Spec.

title: DECIDE: rubric semantics changes (naming 'aliased' value, vendor independence, simpler verdict rule)
description: Judgment calls parked from 2026-07-04 review — human decision, do not auto-apply: (1) naming_alignment 'weak' spans 0%-50% named: tool-integration (0/8 named, industry says function-calling/MCP) gets the same label as security-sandbox (5/10 named). Add 4th value 'aliased' (named==0, aliased>=1 = strongest rename signal), keep 'none' for all-unnamed, compute strong/weak over named/(named+aliased). (2) 'verified' reachable from one vendor's own docs (2xT2=8, no independence requirement); in tool-integration 5/7 entries are vendor-authored. Options: require >=1 practitioner-side entry (T3-T5) or entries from >=2 orgs. (3) Simpler verdict rule '>=1 T1-T3 entry AND >=3 total entries' reproduces ALL 10 current verdicts exactly — could replace the 0-45 score, or keep score as secondary detail and lead with tier counts. (4) Consider requiring mechanism_quote on named T1/T2 entries too — Microsoft org-readiness entry in readiness-assessment.yaml is a name collision that bypassed the same-practice gate; also one-entry-per-author rule (Harper Reed counted T1+T4 in progressive-enhancement, 7/23 pts) and cross-file duplicate-URL warn report (spec-kit/kiro/harper.blog reused across 4 files; 57% of developer-lifecycle's score is shared-pool).
close_reason: All four decisions approved and merged in PR #33 (strict-generation rules)

title: Audit PR3: honesty pass on example READMEs — remove phantom implementations (R1)
description: From AUDIT-RECOMMENDATIONS.md. 14 READMEs claim files/commands/transcripts that do not exist: 10 experiments/examples dirs (workflow-orchestration, security-orchestration, review-automation, handoff-protocols, incident-automation, pipeline-synthesis, testing-orchestration, test-promotion, feedback-flywheel + sections gap) and 4 examples dirs (debt-forecasting, drift-remediation, policy-generation, codified-rules). Use dependency-migration README as spec-compliant template; add 4 spec-required sections to experimental READMEs. Acceptance: guards pass, PR merged.

title: Reconcile the July 10 OpenAI API spend
description: Reconcile the historical July 10 API spend from OpenAI Usage Dashboard Activity and Cost exports by API key, model, and line item. This is accounting follow-up only: model-backed pattern evaluation is now local-only through a signed-in Codex client, and the repository must not add an evaluator API key, weekly API budget, or hosted paid-research path.
acceptance_criteria: The July 10 spend is reconciled by API key, model, and line item; the result records which historical runs produced the charges; no new evaluator API credential, auto-recharge decision, canary dispatch, or GitHub-hosted model run is required.
notes: Historical accounting only. Do not add funds, configure an evaluator key, define a weekly API budget, or dispatch any verification model run. PR #70 contains the retained already-paid evidence; future pattern evaluation uses the signed-in local Codex workflow after PR #70 is resolved.

title: Audit PR5: triage orphaned docs/ island (R4)
description: From AUDIT-RECOMMENDATIONS.md. Link claude-code-guide.md + ai-development-best-practices.md from a README Resources section; delete blog/announcement drafts (move to blog repo is out of scope here — record decision); migrate pattern-ideas.md Pizza Slice Teams into experiments/NOTES.md then delete; delete/fold docs/specs.md + docs/examples/* into spec-driven-development; decide agentic-cicd.md (lower-disruption choice). Acceptance: guards pass, PR merged.

title: Enable native security controls and complete dependency coverage
description: Enable secret scanning, push protection, validity/non-provider scanning where supported, and CodeQL default setup; add dependency review/audits; cover nested npm/Python manifests reproducibly; group Dependabot updates; retire the unused Anthropic review workflow and stale failure issue.
close_reason: Merged PR #83: native security controls, dependency review/audits, exact Actions policy, CodeQL, and retired provider workflow

title: Promote Progressive Disclosure from experiments to main patterns
description:
## Goal
Promote the **Progressive Disclosure** experimental pattern to a stable, top-level pattern.

## References
- `pattern-spec.md` (required template + naming/anchor rules)
- `PATTERN_MIGRATION_GUIDE.md` (canonical naming + anchor conventions)

> Note: `PATTERN_RENAME_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` was not found in the repo root; follow the naming rules above.

## Pattern Content Promotion
- Promote the pattern narrative currently in `experiments/README.md` (`### Progressive Disclosure`) into main `README.md` under `# Development Patterns`.
- Ensure the promoted pattern section conforms to the `pattern-spec.md` structure:
  - **Maturity**, **Description**, **Related Patterns** (hyperlinked)
  - Implementation content + at least one **Anti-pattern**
  - Add/ensure a **Complete Implementation** link (create `examples/progressive-disclosure/` if needed)
- Assign maturity/type (expected: **Intermediate**, **Development**).
- Reconcile dependencies so the **main** reference table does not depend on experimental-only patterns (e.g., `Context Optimization`). If needed, keep experimental patterns as non-dependency “Related Patterns” links instead.

## Root README.md Updates (`README.md`)
- Add a new row to **Complete Pattern Reference** table:
  - Pattern: `Progressive Disclosure`
  - Maturity/Type/Description/Dependencies (dependencies must be patterns in main `README.md`)
  - Place in the Development section near `Codified Rules` / `Context Persistence` / `Custom Commands`.
- Add the full **Progressive Disclosure** section under `# Development Patterns`.
- Update **Pattern Dependencies & Implementation Order** (`graph TD` block) if Progressive Disclosure changes recommended sequencing.
- Update contextual guidance sections if applicable:
  - `### Context-Based Pattern Selection` (Development Teams)
  - `### Project Type Recommendations`
  - `### Team Size Considerations`
- Update pattern count badges after updating tests via `python3 scripts/update-pattern-count.py` (updates `README.md` and `index.html`).

## Cross-Reference Updates
- Remove Progressive Disclosure from `experiments/README.md` reference table and remove its full pattern section.
- Update repo-wide references:
  - `README.md`: convert the existing **Progressive Disclosure** mention in the `Context Persistence` principles list into a hyperlink to `#progressive-disclosure`.
  - `experiments/examples/centralized-rules/sync-strategy/example-central-repo/base/universal-rules.md`: evaluate whether to add a link/anchor reference to the promoted pattern.
- Update **Related Patterns** in other main patterns that should reference Progressive Disclosure (edit the relevant sections in `README.md`):
  - [Codified Rules](README.md#codified-rules)
  - [Context Persistence](README.md#context-persistence)
  - [Event Automation](README.md#event-automation) once promoted
  - [Custom Commands](README.md#custom-commands) once promoted
  - [Centralized Rules](README.md#centralized-rules) once promoted
- Repo-wide search to update any remaining links:
  - Search for `Progressive Disclosure`, `#progressive-disclosure`, and `experiments/README.md#progressive-disclosure`.

## Supporting File Updates
- Tests / validation lists:
  - `tests/conftest.py`: add **Progressive Disclosure** to `EXPECTED_PATTERNS` and `PATTERN_CATEGORIES` (Development).
- Docs/config references (update if present):
  - `CLAUDE.md`
  - `.ai/` rules/prompts
- Verify workflows/tests:
  - `.github/workflows/pattern-validation.yml`
  - Run local suite (at minimum): `pytest -q`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Progressive Disclosure is documented in `README.md` (table + full pattern section) and removed from `experiments/README.md`.
- A complete implementation link exists (and `examples/progressive-disclosure/` exists, if added as part of promotion).
- Root README cross-references (e.g., `Context Persistence` principle list) link to `#progressive-disclosure`.
- No remaining links reference `experiments/README.md#progressive-disclosure`; full `pattern-validation.yml` test suite passes.

title: Deep repo consistency analysis vs pattern-spec.md (patterns, experiments, cruft)
description: Analyze README patterns + experiments for spec compliance, hyperlink/table integrity, naming rules, length limits; sweep for cruft/prunable files. Deliverable: recommendations only, no changes.
close_reason: Deep analysis complete: 97 adversarially-verified findings synthesized into recommendations; report delivered, no repo changes made

title: Audit PR6: patterns.yaml related-list sync + guard test; meta-layer fixes (R8+R9)
description: From AUDIT-RECOMMENDATIONS.md. R8: reconcile 9+ divergent yaml related lists to README canonical; add guard test in tests/test_yaml_readme_sync.py. R9: fix pattern-validation.yml /xaudit text + notify-failure checklist; make claude-code-review.yml prompt repo-specific; add configure-repository-rules.py to evidence-validation triggers; fix/retire tests/run_all_tests.py; regenerate tests/README.md; document scripts/README.md verification toolchain + fix false claims; delete pre-commit-pattern-count.sh; decide 4 orphaned verification scripts; align generate-audit-prompt.py command; handle .claude/prompts/xaudit-experiments.md. Acceptance: guards pass, PR merged.
close_reason: Completed the audit meta-layer corrections across merged PR #86 and the validated follow-up, including documentation, workflow prompts, tooling retirement, and guard tests.

title: Run full verification across the main and experimental catalog
description: Make weekly and on-demand verification cover every selected main and experimental pattern through deterministic bounded research batches, fresh per-batch validation, trusted aggregation, and one draft PR per run. Preserve in-flight de-duplication and fail-closed provenance/scope guards.
acceptance_criteria: A workflow dispatched from main creates one isolated evidence unit for every available main and experimental pattern plus discovery, validates every unit and aggregate candidate fail-closed, and publishes at most one draft PR; weekly/on-demand behavior is documented and tested.
notes:
Replaced the failed monolithic full run with plan-hashed per-pattern matrices (24 main + 25 experimental), per-unit validation, trusted assembly, retry-safe publishing, and OpenAI/Anthropic isolation. Awaiting merge to main and a monitored full OpenAI dispatch.
Full run 29123202495 proved 49/49 research coverage but failed 11 immediate validation re-fetches because dynamic pages changed digest/redirect content between jobs. Follow-up branch moves authoritative URL/quote hydration into trusted post-model steps for both providers, binds hydrated bytes in manifests, and keeps weekly rechecks. Full local suite: 279 passed.
Main run 29127511821 was cancelled after trusted hydration hit a single 20-second ReadTimeout fetching an OpenAI source; 51 remaining jobs were stopped to preserve credits and no PR was published. Follow-up branch codex/hydration-retries adds shared bounded transient retries (3 attempts, 30-second timeout, 1/2-second backoff), exact retry classification, observable warnings, and fail-closed atomicity tests before another full dispatch.
Corrected full run 29128373501 cleared the prior hydration regression (developer-lifecycle passed) but was cancelled after OpenAI returned quota exceeded across six concurrent units; 6/49 research units succeeded and no PR was published. OPENAI_EVIDENCE_MODEL is now gpt-5.6-luna, the official cost-sensitive high-volume model. Anthropic fallback run 29129076453 was also cancelled after the stored key returned authentication_failed on every probe; external WIF task 06l remains open. Adding a single selected-provider preflight before matrix expansion; external OpenAI funding is tracked in d2q.
Cost audit found at least 8.22M logged OpenAI tokens today, dominated by run 29123202495. All 49 raw unit artifacts from that run remain available until 2026-07-17; recover them with current trusted hydration/validation instead of making another model call.
Recovered all 48 full-run evidence units plus discovery from retained run 29123202495 artifacts, and folded the separately trusted Agent Readiness unit from run 29117515902 into one aggregate review. No provider calls were made. Current trusted validation passes all 49 files; live quote checks pass; 317 fast tests pass; aggregate result is 45 verified and 4 weak with zero pending patterns. Publishing on verify/refresh-29123202495-1 as one draft PR.
close_reason: Completed: the main-branch workflow now supports bounded weekly/on-demand full-catalog units with trusted aggregation, and recovered source artifacts were published as one fully green draft PR #70 covering all 49 patterns without another provider call.

title: Isolate root-level pytest collection
description: Make a plain repository-root pytest invocation collect supported suites without nested conftest collisions or spec-driven import-path failures. Current official tests pass when scoped to tests/, while an unscoped root invocation imports experiments/examples/test-promotion/conftest.py for repository tests and cannot import the spec-driven example module.
close_reason: Merged PR #83: root pytest collection is isolated to the supported tests directory

title: Bound OpenAI verification spend and make retries resumable
description: Add repository-enforced per-worker weighted token budgets, fail-fast/low-concurrency execution, an explicit full-catalog confirmation gate, and same-run research checkpoints so Actions retries do not repeat successful paid model work. Document the spend envelope and prove it with tests before another paid run.
notes: Implemented a 300,000 weighted-token stop per OpenAI worker (output weighted 6.25x), low reasoning, cost-sensitive model allowlist, explicit full/high-cost acknowledgements, max-parallel 1, fail-fast research, 12-search instruction cap, run-summary envelope, and same-run fixed-path checkpoints for both providers. Local non-slow suite: 317 passed.

title: Harden pattern adoption validator and scheduled verification
description: Implement deterministic on-demand/weekly validation, secure evidence refresh/publish boundaries, strict versioned evidence schema and migration, auditable search provenance, stronger independence/adoption scoring, accurate status reporting, Actions hardening, and required repository checks.
close_reason: Completed in PRs #38 and #54: validator, schedules, trust boundaries, migrations, repository rules, tests, and live fail-closed verification are active. External Anthropic WIF setup remains separately tracked by ai-development-patterns-06l.

title: Audit PR4: rewrite pattern-spec.md drift + formatting normalization (R2+R10)
description: From AUDIT-RECOMMENDATIONS.md. R2: fix spec heading-depth rule, section ordering, dead length guidelines, related-links limit, document patterns.yaml, experiments model, validation checklist, Antipattern spelling. R10: rename Opaque Agent Runs -> Opaque Runs and Voting Theater -> False Consensus; extend validate-pattern-names.py to lint antipattern names; normalize Maturity line break, anti-pattern label markup, examples-link labels, Related Patterns lens links, Operations implementation section labels, trim oversized code block, header-row Type convention. Acceptance: guards pass, PR merged.

title: Interactive patterns website generated from README.md
description: Build an editorial-design patterns browser page (upgrade index.html) that shows all patterns as clickable boxes with brief descriptions and rich in-page detail. Source of record is README.md: a build-time Python parser (scripts/generate-patterns-data.py) extracts the Complete Pattern Reference table + per-pattern sections + dependency diagram into assets/patterns-data.js, regenerated in CI before GitHub Pages deploy. Acceptance: (1) parser produces data for all patterns in README table; (2) page renders grouped grid + filters + search + detail modal + dependency diagram; (3) existing test_diagram.py and update-pattern-count.py contracts preserved; (4) new tests verify README->data sync with --check mode; (5) deploy-pages.yml regenerates data on every push.
close_reason: Shipped interactive patterns website (AI Development Patterns by Paul Duvall): README-generated cards + detail modal + branded dependency diagram; auto-refresh wiring (build.sh, deploy, --check gate, pre-commit, test guard)

title: Make OpenAI the default verification research provider
description: Add an explicit provider selector to Pattern Verification & Discovery. Scheduled and default manual runs use the official OpenAI Codex GitHub Action with a dedicated OpenAI Platform API key; Anthropic remains an explicit opt-in path. Preserve read-only research credentials, fixed-path candidate artifacts, deterministic exact-scope/provenance validation, secret scanning, and separate scoped publication.
design: Use separate conditional OpenAI and Anthropic research jobs so OpenAI does not receive Anthropic WIF id-token permission. Pin every action to an immutable SHA. Do not silently fall back between providers. Bind the selected model into evidence verifier provenance.
acceptance_criteria: Workflow dispatch offers openai and anthropic with openai default; weekly/monthly schedules select openai; OpenAI uses a pinned official action and CLI, gpt-5.6-terra medium, native web search, a candidate-scoped permission profile, a private workspace, and a dedicated unprivileged OS account; all research-user processes and the API proxy terminate before trusted export; Anthropic guard/WIF path remains intact; search and verifier metadata bind provider/model/contract digest to the immutable run; both paths feed the same fixed candidate/validation/publish trust boundary; structural tests, full pytest, actionlint, and zizmor pass; changes are merged and an on-demand OpenAI run is verified when credentials are configured.
notes: PRs #56-#59 merged the OpenAI-default provider, hardened unprivileged Codex sandbox and runtime home, fixed-path export and deterministic validation, and the skipped-provider publication guard. Live OpenAI run 29113518138 succeeded end to end with gpt-5.6-terra: research, process cleanup, secret and path scans, exact-scope and provenance validation, semantic URL and hash checks, publication, evidence-gap reconciliation, and trusted validation dispatch. It created draft evidence PR #60 and issue #61. Trusted default-branch run 29113730196 passed and attached the successful Trusted evidence checks status to the candidate revision. Local and PR gates passed; the unrelated optional Claude review still reports its pre-existing invalid Anthropic key.
close_reason: Completed in merged PRs #56-#59 and verified end to end by OpenAI run 29113518138, draft evidence PR #60, and trusted validation run 29113730196.

title: Pin verified peer addresses during evidence retrieval
description: Harden scripts/evidence_content.py against DNS rebinding between validate_public_url() resolution and the later requests connection. Preserve redirect revalidation, TLS hostname verification, public-only address policy, response-size bounds, and tests for IPv4/IPv6 plus redirects.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence fetches connect only to an address already proven global for that exact redirect hop while TLS still validates the original hostname; tests demonstrate a DNS answer changing to a private address cannot reach the private peer.
close_reason: Merged PR #83: evidence retrieval pins verified peer addresses and rejects DNS rebinding/unsafe redirects

title: Promote Centralized Rules from experiments to main patterns
description:
## Goal
Promote the **Centralized Rules** experimental pattern to a stable, top-level pattern.

## References
- `pattern-spec.md` (required template + naming/anchor rules)
- `PATTERN_MIGRATION_GUIDE.md` (canonical naming + anchor conventions)

> Note: `PATTERN_RENAME_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` was not found in the repo root; follow the naming rules above.

## Pattern Content Promotion
- Move example implementation: `experiments/examples/centralized-rules/` → `examples/centralized-rules/`.
- Review and update internal links inside the moved example docs (e.g., any `../../README.md#...` references) so they resolve to the new canonical section in root `README.md`.
- Ensure the promoted pattern section conforms to the `pattern-spec.md` structure:
  - **Maturity**, **Description**, **Related Patterns** (hyperlinked)
  - Implementation content + at least one **Anti-pattern**
  - **Complete Implementation** link(s) to `examples/centralized-rules/` subdirectories
- Confirm maturity/type are set appropriately (expected: **Advanced**, **Operations**).

## Root README.md Updates (`README.md`)
- Add a new row to **Complete Pattern Reference** table:
  - Pattern: `Centralized Rules`
  - Maturity/Type/Description/Dependencies (ensure dependencies point only to patterns in main `README.md`)
  - Place under the appropriate Operations sub-category row (likely **Security & Compliance**).
- Add a full **Centralized Rules** pattern section under `# Operations Patterns` (likely within `## Security & Compliance Patterns`) by promoting/adapting content from `experiments/README.md`.
- Update **Pattern Dependencies & Implementation Order** (`graph TD` block) to include Centralized Rules and its prerequisites.
- Update contextual guidance sections if applicable:
  - `### Context-Based Pattern Selection` (Enterprise/Production)
  - `### Project Type Recommendations` (Enterprise)
  - `### Team Size Considerations` (Multi-team orgs)
- Update pattern count badges after updating tests via `python3 scripts/update-pattern-count.py` (updates `README.md` and `index.html`).

## Cross-Reference Updates
- Remove Centralized Rules from:
  - `experiments/README.md` reference table and its full pattern section.
  - `experiments/examples/README.md` example catalog table.
- Update **Related Patterns** in other main patterns that should reference Centralized Rules (edit the relevant sections in `README.md`):
  - [Codified Rules](README.md#codified-rules)
  - [Policy Generation](README.md#policy-generation)
  - [Security Orchestration](README.md#security-orchestration)
  - [Security Sandbox](README.md#security-sandbox) (if relevant)
  - [Progressive Disclosure](README.md#progressive-disclosure) once promoted
- Repo-wide search to update any remaining links:
  - Search for `Centralized Rules`, `#centralized-rules`, and `experiments/README.md#centralized-rules`.

## Supporting File Updates
- Tests / validation lists:
  - `tests/conftest.py`: add **Centralized Rules** to `EXPECTED_PATTERNS` and `PATTERN_CATEGORIES` (Operations).
- Verify workflows/tests still pass with new paths:
  - `.github/workflows/pattern-validation.yml` (no change expected, but confirm)
  - Run local suite (at minimum): `pytest -q`
- Check for references in config/docs:
  - `CLAUDE.md` (update if it references the pattern)
  - `.ai/` rule/prompt files (update if they reference experimental paths)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Centralized Rules is documented in `README.md` (table + full pattern section) and removed from `experiments/README.md`.
- Example implementation lives at `examples/centralized-rules/` and no longer under `experiments/examples/`.
- All repo references point to `README.md#centralized-rules` (no remaining `experiments/README.md#centralized-rules` links).
- `python3 scripts/update-pattern-count.py` produces no diff after changes; full `pattern-validation.yml` test suite passes.

title: Epic: Repo consistency + link health alignment
description:
Audit findings (2026-01-31):

- README passes pattern-spec + hyperlink tests (including slow external link check).
- 65 broken internal/relative links across 20 non-README markdown files (mostly stale anchors/paths from pre-migration naming).
- Stable example directories are named correctly, but almost all example READMEs still use pre-migration pattern names (e.g., 'Rules as Code', 'AI Security Sandbox', etc.) and contain stale links.
- Experimental docs/examples still reference old pattern names/anchors and some example READMEs reference missing files.
- CLAUDE.md repo tree is out of date vs current directories.
- index.html drifts from README (missing patterns + category mismatch).

Goal: bring docs/examples/tests/scripts into alignment with pattern-spec.md conventions (names, links, structure) and ensure link integrity repo-wide.

Acceptance:
- All markdown internal/relative links validate repo-wide.
- Examples/docs use current pattern names + anchors.
- CI/tests cover links beyond README.

title: Fix placeholder links in pattern-spec.md
description:
pattern-spec.md currently contains markdown links that are meant to be examples/placeholders (e.g., [Pattern Name](#pattern-name-anchor), examples/pattern-name/). These render as broken links in link-audit tooling and undermine the spec’s own link-integrity guidance.

Scope:
- pattern-spec.md: ensure example link syntax is presented as code/escaped text (not clickable broken links).

Notes:
- Link audit currently reports 4 broken internal/relative links in pattern-spec.md (missing internal anchor + missing file).
acceptance_criteria:
- pattern-spec.md contains no broken internal/relative links when running a repo-wide markdown link audit (excluding code blocks).
- Example link formats remain clear (prefer code formatting/backticks or escaping).

title: Update experiments/examples/README.md to current experimental catalog
description:
experiments/examples/README.md appears to be from a pre-migration state and references:
- Experimental pattern names that no longer exist (e.g., AI Workflow Orchestration, Comprehensive AI Testing Strategy).
- Example directories that do not exist (e.g., human-ai-handoff/, comprehensive-ai-testing-strategy/).
- Broken links to stable examples (e.g., ../../examples/specification-driven-development/).

Scope:
- Rewrite the table to match the current experiments/README.md pattern list and the actual directory names under experiments/examples/.
- Fix links to stable examples to use existing paths and current anchors.
acceptance_criteria:
- experiments/examples/README.md accurately reflects experiments/examples/* directory names.
- All links in experiments/examples/README.md resolve (repo-wide markdown link audit shows 0 broken links).
- Terminology matches the current experimental pattern names (no legacy AI-prefixed names unless explicitly called out as history).

title: Complete asynchronous-research example directory or remove dead links
description:
experiments/examples/asynchronous-research/README.md links to multiple files that are not present in the repo (setup/*.md, example-research-tasks/*/prompt.md, automation/summarize-workflow.yml). The directory currently contains only README.md.

Scope options:
A) Add the referenced files/directories with minimal working content, consistent with the README.
B) If the full implementation is not ready, update README.md to remove/replace links and clearly mark the example as a stub.

Goal: avoid dead links and keep experimental examples trustworthy.
acceptance_criteria:
- Repo-wide markdown link audit shows 0 missing-file links for experiments/examples/asynchronous-research/README.md.
- The directory either contains the referenced files or the README no longer references them.
- Example directory passes ExampleDirectoryValidator expectations (README present, no obvious dead references).

title: Update experimental example READMEs to current names + anchors
description:
Several tracked experimental example READMEs and example docs still reference legacy pattern names/anchors (ai-*, AI-Driven, etc.) and/or legacy pattern names in headings.

Known broken-link files from link audit:
- experiments/examples/custom-commands/*.md (links to ../../README.md#ai-driven-refactoring, #specification-driven-development, etc.)
- experiments/examples/handoff-protocols/README.md (links to ../../README.md#human-ai-handoff-protocol)
- experiments/examples/workflow-orchestration/README.md (links to ../../README.md#ai-workflow-orchestration)
- experiments/examples/review-automation/README.md (links to ../../README.md#ai-review-automation)

Scope:
- Update these docs to reference current canonical pattern anchors in README and experiments/README.
- Update H1s where they still use legacy names (e.g., “AI Review Automation Implementation” -> “Review Automation Implementation”).
acceptance_criteria:
- All tracked experimental example docs use current pattern names.
- Repo-wide markdown link audit shows 0 missing-target-anchor failures for experiments/examples/**.
- No references to non-existent README anchors (ai-*, specification-driven-development, etc.).

title: Normalize Foundation example READMEs to current pattern names
description:
Tracked example directories for Foundation patterns use correct directory names, but the README titles/content are still pre-migration (AI-prefixed names, old anchors) and include broken links.

Affected dirs:
- examples/codified-rules (README title: “Rules as Code Example”; broken links to old anchors)
- examples/security-sandbox (README title: “AI Security Sandbox Implementation”; deeper path issues handled in separate sandbox task)
- examples/developer-lifecycle (README title: “AI Developer Lifecycle Example”)
- examples/tool-integration (README title: “AI Tool Integration - Complete Implementation”; broken links to ../../sandbox/ and old anchors)
- examples/issue-generation (README title: “AI Issue Generation Examples”)

Scope:
- Update each example README (and any adjacent docs in the directory) to use current canonical pattern names as per README/pattern-spec.
- Update links to README anchors to current anchors (no ai-* legacy anchors).
- Remove/replace references to non-existent ../../sandbox/ paths (use correct example paths).

Note: Security Sandbox example path correctness is tracked separately (ai-development-patterns-zqc.8).
acceptance_criteria:
- Each listed examples/* README uses current canonical pattern name in its H1.
- Repo-wide markdown link audit shows no broken internal/relative links in these example docs.
- No remaining links to non-existent README anchors (ai-*, rules-as-code, specification-driven-development, etc.).

title: Normalize Development example READMEs to current pattern names
description:
Most Development pattern examples still use pre-migration names in their README H1s and cross-links.

Affected dirs (tracked):
- examples/spec-driven-development (README title: “Specification Driven Development”; broken links to old anchors and missing ../../sandbox/)
- examples/planned-implementation (README title: “AI Plan-First Development”; links to #ai-plan-first-development)
- examples/atomic-decomposition (README title: “Atomic Task Decomposition Example”; links to #atomic-task-decomposition)
- examples/parallel-agents (README title: “Parallelized AI Coding Agents”; links to #parallelized-ai-coding-agents)
- examples/context-persistence (README title: “AI Context Persistence Implementation”)
- examples/observable-development (README title: “Observable AI Development Implementation”)
- examples/guided-refactoring (README title: “AI-Driven Refactoring Example”)
- examples/automated-traceability (README title: “AI-Driven Traceability Implementation”)
- examples/error-resolution (README title already matches, but verify links/naming within)

Scope:
- Update README titles and any internal references/links to use current canonical names/anchors from root README.
- Remove/replace any ../../sandbox/ references with paths that exist in this repo.
- Ensure docs within these directories (e.g., markdown-iteration-techniques.md, function-level-decomposition.md) link to current anchors.
acceptance_criteria:
- Each listed examples/* README uses current canonical pattern name in its H1.
- Repo-wide markdown link audit shows no broken internal/relative links in these example docs.
- No remaining links to non-existent README anchors (ai-*, specification-driven-development, atomic-task-decomposition, parallelized-*, etc.).

title: Normalize Operations example READMEs to current pattern names
description:
Operations pattern examples also contain legacy naming in their README H1s.

Affected dirs (tracked):
- examples/policy-generation (README title: “Policy-as-Code Generation Implementation”)
- examples/security-orchestration (README title: “Security Scanning Orchestration Implementation”)
- examples/baseline-management (README title: “Performance Baseline Management Implementation”)

Scope:
- Update README titles and internal references to use current canonical names (Policy Generation, Security Orchestration, Baseline Management).
- Ensure any cross-links to README anchors use current anchors.
acceptance_criteria:
- Each listed examples/* README uses current canonical pattern name in its H1.
- Repo-wide markdown link audit shows no broken internal/relative links in these example docs.
- No remaining legacy pattern names in headings unless explicitly documented as historical.

title: Register pytest markers (slow) to remove warnings
description:
Running pytest currently emits PytestUnknownMarkWarning for pytest.mark.slow used in tests/test_links.py.

Scope:
- Add pytest configuration (pytest.ini or pyproject.toml) to register the slow marker (and any other custom markers in use).
- Ensure CI commands using -m slow / -m "not slow" continue to work without warnings.
acceptance_criteria:
- pytest runs without PytestUnknownMarkWarning for slow marker.
- test_links.py still supports selecting slow vs not slow.

title: Add repo-wide markdown link validation (docs/examples/experiments)
description:
Current CI/test coverage validates links mainly in README.md. A repo-wide audit found 65 broken internal/relative links across 20 non-README markdown files.

Scope:
- Add a new pytest test (or extend existing link checker) to validate internal anchors + relative file links across markdown files in:
  - docs/
  - examples/
  - experiments/
  - pattern-spec.md and other top-level docs
- Exclude code blocks and known placeholder/example snippets.
- Integrate into GitHub Actions (pattern-validation.yml) so broken links are caught in PRs.

Sequencing note:
- This should likely be enabled after existing broken links are fixed, otherwise it will fail immediately.
acceptance_criteria:
- A new test exists that validates internal/relative links across non-README markdown.
- GitHub Actions runs the test and fails on broken links.
- The test has reasonable exclusions (code blocks, explicit placeholder examples) and low flakiness.

title: Normalize Python invocation in GitHub Actions workflows
description:
In .github/workflows/pattern-validation.yml, most jobs use python3, but the quality-gates job uses plain python for pytest invocations. This can be brittle across runner images.

Scope:
- Use python3 consistently in workflow steps.
- Optionally ensure the same virtualenv/dependencies are used for quality-gates steps.

Goal: avoid environment-dependent failures.
acceptance_criteria:
- pattern-validation.yml uses consistent python3 invocation.
- Workflow continues to run the same tests as before.

title: Harden example-validation against local stray directories
description:
Local test run showed Example Code Validation failing due to directories that exist on disk but are not tracked in git (e.g., examples/ai-issue-generation/, examples/ai-context-persistence/, experiments/examples/ai-event-automation/), each missing README.md.

These directories are likely leftovers from earlier migrations or local experimentation; they are empty or contain only ignored files, so git status stays clean, but ExampleDirectoryValidator still scans them.

Options:
- Update ExampleDirectoryValidator to only validate git-tracked example directories (or only directories containing a README.md).
- Or document a cleanup step / add a guard that warns when such stray dirs exist.

Goal: make local validation predictable and aligned with CI behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
- Running tests/run_all_tests.py on a working tree with stray empty example dirs does not fail Example Code Validation (or fails with a clear actionable message).
- CI behavior remains unchanged.

title: Fix docs/claude-code-guide.md TOC anchors
description:
docs/claude-code-guide.md has a manually-maintained Table of Contents whose anchors do not match the document headings (likely due to emoji in headings causing the TOC to include a leading hyphen / variation selector). Link audit reports 10 missing internal anchors at the top of the file.

Scope:
- docs/claude-code-guide.md: update TOC links to match actual heading anchors (or adjust headings so anchors match TOC).
- Also convert the single external YouTube link that uses http:// to https://.

Notes:
- Link audit: 10 broken internal anchors in docs/claude-code-guide.md.
acceptance_criteria:
- TOC links navigate correctly within docs/claude-code-guide.md on GitHub.
- Repo-wide markdown link audit reports 0 broken internal anchors for docs/claude-code-guide.md.
- No remaining http:// external links for that YouTube entry.

title: Update CLAUDE.md to match current repo structure
description:
CLAUDE.md repository overview lists directories that do not exist in the current repo (sandbox/, specs/, policies/, monitoring/, ops/, pipelines/, deployment/) and includes line-number references that appear stale.

Scope:
- CLAUDE.md: refresh the Repository Structure tree to match the actual repo layout.
- Ensure pattern naming references align with current README/pattern-spec naming (no AI-prefixed pattern names unless intentionally describing legacy).

Acceptance should be measurable by reading the file; no code changes beyond documentation.
acceptance_criteria:
- CLAUDE.md file tree matches the actual top-level directories in the repo.
- Any referenced paths in CLAUDE.md exist.
- Pattern name references match current README anchors.

title: Align index.html with README pattern taxonomy
description:
index.html appears to be a GitHub Pages landing page but currently drifts from README:
- It omits several patterns entirely (e.g., Planned Implementation, Choice Generation, Constrained Generation, Guided Architecture, Error Resolution).
- It classifies Context Persistence as a Foundation pattern even though README categorizes it under Development.
- Pattern count badge is hard-coded (22) and may drift if patterns change.

Scope:
- index.html: update pattern lists/taxonomy to match README (or explicitly mark as a curated subset).
- Consider making pattern count and lists generated or clearly documented to avoid drift.
acceptance_criteria:
- index.html pattern lists are consistent with README (either complete or clearly stated as intentionally partial).
- Pattern categories in index.html match README taxonomy.
- Pattern count badge cannot silently drift (auto-generated or documented update step).

title: Reconcile PATTERN_MIGRATION_GUIDE.md and migration scripts
description:
PATTERN_MIGRATION_GUIDE.md and scripts/*renames*.sh appear to describe/encode a migration to two-word naming, but multiple parts conflict with current README/pattern-spec:
- Guide references pattern names/anchors that no longer exist (e.g., Spec-First) while README uses Spec-Driven Development.
- Several examples/experiments still contain legacy names even though the guide claims repository references were updated.

Scope:
- Decide whether PATTERN_MIGRATION_GUIDE.md is historical (archive it) or should reflect the current canonical names.
- Update or retire scripts/apply-pattern-renames.sh and scripts/apply-experimental-renames.sh accordingly.
- Update scripts/README.md examples that reference the old pattern count (21) to current count.

Goal: remove confusion for readers and prevent future drift.
acceptance_criteria:
- Migration guide either (a) matches the current canonical naming/anchors, or (b) is clearly marked as historical and moved/segmented accordingly.
- Any referenced “new names” in the guide exist in README.
- Migration scripts are either updated to current names or clearly archived with warnings.
- scripts/README.md examples reflect current pattern count.

title: Update docs/introducing-ai-development-patterns.md to current pattern names
description:
docs/introducing-ai-development-patterns.md uses pre-migration pattern names (AI Readiness Assessment, Rules as Code, AI Security Sandbox, Specification Driven Development, Progressive AI Enhancement, etc.) and includes bare URLs.

Scope:
- docs/introducing-ai-development-patterns.md: update pattern names to match current canonical names in README/pattern-spec.
- Link pattern mentions to README anchors.
- Convert bare repo URLs to standard markdown links.

Goal: keep introductory content consistent with the current pattern catalog.
acceptance_criteria:
- docs/introducing-ai-development-patterns.md uses current canonical pattern names.
- Pattern mentions are hyperlinked to README anchors.
- No bare GitHub repo URLs remain (use markdown links).\n- Repo-wide markdown link audit shows no broken links in this file.

title: Align docs/ai-development-best-practices.md with canonical pattern names
description:
docs/ai-development-best-practices.md is framed as a best-practices list, but it references several patterns using legacy names (e.g., Rules as Code, Specification Driven Development, etc.) and includes legacy/experimental names that no longer match the current catalog.

Scope:
- Update references to stable patterns to use current names (Codified Rules, Spec-Driven Development, etc.).
- Where a best practice is directly derived from a pattern, hyperlink to README (or experiments/README for experimental patterns).
- Keep the best-practices voice, but ensure nomenclature matches the repository.

Note: do not try to force every bullet into a pattern; only update where it clearly maps.
acceptance_criteria:
- Stable pattern references use current canonical names.
- Mapped pattern references are hyperlinked to the appropriate README/experiments anchors.
- Repo-wide markdown link audit shows no broken links in this file.

title: Align Security Sandbox blog + example paths
description:
docs/ai-security-sandbox-blog.md and examples/security-sandbox/ appear out of sync:
- Blog uses the legacy pattern name “AI Security Sandbox” and references a root-level sandbox/ directory and ./sandbox/ai-sandbox.sh, but the repo uses examples/security-sandbox/.
- examples/security-sandbox/README.md references files that are not present (e.g., security-monitor.py, init-sandbox.sh) and uses legacy naming.
- examples/security-sandbox/docker-compose.ai-sandbox.yml references sandbox/Dockerfile.ai-sandbox and ./sandbox/* paths that do not exist.

Scope:
- Decide the canonical location for the sandbox implementation (likely examples/security-sandbox/).
- Update blog instructions + links accordingly.
- Fix examples/security-sandbox/README.md file list to match actual files.
- Fix docker-compose paths so the example is runnable (or clearly documented placeholders).

This is a high-impact consistency fix because Security Sandbox is a foundational pattern.
acceptance_criteria:
- docs/ai-security-sandbox-blog.md references the correct implementation paths in this repo.
- examples/security-sandbox/README.md accurately lists files that exist.
- Security sandbox compose files reference paths that exist in-repo.
- Repo-wide markdown link audit shows no broken links for the blog and the example README.

title: Fix broken links + legacy anchors in experiments/README.md
description:
experiments/README.md still contains legacy links to main README anchors and missing example paths:
- Broken internal link: #monitoring--intelligence
- Legacy anchors: ../README.md#ai-choice-generation, ../README.md#parallelized-ai-coding-agents
- Missing paths: examples/custom-ai-commands/* (should likely be examples/custom-commands/*)

Scope:
- experiments/README.md: update internal anchor links and convert any legacy references to current anchors.
- Update example-path references to match the actual experiments/examples directory structure.
- Consider hyperlinking Dependencies in the experimental reference table for consistency with pattern-spec expectations.
acceptance_criteria:
- Repo-wide markdown link audit shows 0 broken internal/relative links in experiments/README.md.
- No references to non-existent README anchors (ai-*, parallelized-*, etc.).
- Example paths in the file exist.
